page: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor. The system is configured with 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz under a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong memory subsystem and highly capable single-core processing. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance achieves top-tier performance, placing in the top 10%. However, multi-core CPU performance falls into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests for both large-block read and write operations rank in the top 25%, which aligns with its strong PassMark memory score. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all perform within average parameters, while medium-model LLM inference prompt processing shows weaker relative performance.

The shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, predictable multi-core execution, as reflected in the average multi-core benchmark results. However, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial 1600 GB NVMe SSD storage bundle provide qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads that do not constantly saturate the CPU. This profile is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers like Redis, memory-intensive data processing, and general-purpose web serving.
description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor with 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and a 2.0 GHz clock speed under a shared CPU allocation. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of RAM and 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth, though multi-core performance and database operations remain average. The shared CPU model presents a tradeoff for sustained multi-threaded workloads, but the high memory density and bundled NVMe storage offer qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications. This instance is suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive web serving.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-1600s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1600 GB NVMe storage, offering top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-8c-64gb-1600s-amd features 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1600 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: An AMD EPYC instance combining high memory density and fast NVMe storage for memory-intensive and database workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Strong memory read and write bandwidth performance
Suited for database hosting, caching, and memory-intensive applications
